# Верификация фактов из coin-analytics-dialog.md

Адверсариальная проверка пяти несущих фактических утверждений диалога по первичным источникам (GitHub releases, docs.dash.org, gRPC proto, live explorer API, on-chain серии ZecHub). Каждый вердикт: скептик пытался ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ утверждение.

| 5 | DAPI/Dash Platform only exposes CURRENT state for shielded pool metrics — no historical/height-parameterized q… | **CONFIRMED** | high |

## 1. CONFIRMED (high)

**Утверждение.** Dash Platform v4.0 launched Orchard shielded pools (Zcash's Halo 2 zk-SNARK technology) on mainnet, announced by the official @Dashpay X account.

**Evidence.** Attempted refutation; the claim survived. (1) PRIMARY — github.com/dashpay/platform/releases: "Dash Platform v4.0.0" released July 1, 2026, marked Latest (not pre-release), preceded by v4.0.0-rc.1 (Jun 10) and rc.2 (Jun 12). Release notes explicitly describe "a privacy layer for Platform credits built on a fork of the Orchard protocol" using Halo 2, Pallas/Vesta curves, Sinsemilla, with Bech32m address HRP `dash1z` for MAINNET (`tdash1z` testnet) and a 36-byte DashMemo cutting note size ~692→216 bytes. (2) PRIMARY (indirect) — the @Dashpay announcement tweet x.com/Dashpay/status/2078084477746598240, cited by two independent outlets with quoted text "We just launched Orchard on MAINNET. RIGHT NOW!... 1-second confirmations. Full wallet sync in ~20 seconds"; X blocked direct fetch (HTTP 402), but the snowflake ID deterministically decodes to 2026-07-17T11:48:43 UTC, matching the reported announcement date exactly. (3) PRIMARY — dash.org/roadmap lists "Shielded Balances: Dash Platform integrates the Zcash Orchard shielded pool" as the July 2026 milestone (not yet stamped "complete" at fetch time — consistent with a page 2 days stale, and it did not contradict the launch). (4) SECONDARY — crypto-economy.com, cryptopolitan.com, plus Bitget/KuCoin/mpost all independently report mainnet activation on the Evolution chain on July 17, 2026, with Dash CTO Samuel Westrich quoted. Residual gaps: could not read the tweet firsthand, and platform-explorer.com showed "API: disrupted" so no on-chain cross-check; nothing found contradicts the claim. Nuance: the shielded pool covers Platform credits on the Evolution/Platform layer (L2), not the core Dash payment chain, and it is a fork of Orchard, not a Zcash dependency.

**Corrected.** Essentially as stated, with precision: Dash Platform v4.0.0 was released on GitHub July 1, 2026, and its Orchard-based shielded pools — a fork of Zcash's Orchard protocol using the Halo 2 proof system (no trusted setup) — were activated on MAINNET on July 17, 2026, announced that day by the official @Dashpay X account (~11:48 UTC). Two clarifications: the shielded pool applies to Platform credits on the Dash Platform/Evolution layer (not the core Dash L1 payment chain, which keeps CoinJoin), and shielded tokenized assets/stablecoins are planned for a subsequent release, not part of this launch.

**Sources:**
- https://github.com/dashpay/platform/releases (primary — v4.0.0 release, Jul 1 2026, Orchard/Halo2 shielded pool notes)
- https://www.dash.org/roadmap/ (primary — July 2026 'Shielded Balances / Zcash Orchard shielded pool' milestone)
- https://x.com/Dashpay/status/2078084477746598240 (primary — announcement tweet; direct fetch blocked HTTP 402; ID timestamp decoded to 2026-07-17T11:48:43Z)
- https://platform-explorer.com/ (primary — inconclusive, API disrupted)
- https://crypto-economy.com/dash-activates-orchard-privacy-system-using-zcash-tech-to-hide-transactions/ (secondary — quotes the @Dashpay tweet text and date)
- https://www.cryptopolitan.com/dash-launch-zcash-orchard-technology/ (secondary — Jul 17 2026 mainnet activation, cites same tweet URL)
- https://www.bitget.com/asia/news/detail/12560605514495 (secondary — corroborating)
- https://www.kucoin.com/news/insight/DASH/6a5afb33dd913100071c911d (secondary — corroborating)

## 2. PARTIALLY-CONFIRMED (high)

**Утверждение.** Dash Platform DAPI exposes endpoints getShieldedPoolState, getShieldedNotesCount, getMostRecentShieldedAnchor, getShieldedAnchors, and getTotalCreditsInPlatform (per docs.dash.org), and defines five shielded transition types: Shield, Shielded Transfer, Unshield, Shield from Asset Lock, Shielded Withdrawal.

**Evidence.** Endpoints sub-claim CONFIRMED: raw platform.proto on dashpay/platform default branch v4.1-dev defines rpc getTotalCreditsInPlatform (line 78) and six shielded RPCs: getShieldedEncryptedNotes (123), getShieldedAnchors (125), getMostRecentShieldedAnchor (127), getShieldedPoolState (129), getShieldedNotesCount (131), getShieldedNullifiers (133); GitHub code search returns 22 files for getShieldedPoolState incl. rs-drive-abci/src/query/shielded/pool_state/. Raw HTML of docs.dash.org latest dapi-endpoints.html contains a "Shielded Transactions" category with anchors #getshieldedpoolstate, #getshieldednotescount, #getmostrecentshieldedanchor, #getshieldedanchors (plus #getshieldedencryptednotes, #getshieldednullifiers) on dapi-endpoints-platform-endpoints.html, and getTotalCreditsInPlatform under System Info. Caveat: the endpoints appear only in the "latest" docs version; the "stable" platform-endpoints page (fetched 2026-07-19) has no shielded section. Transition-types sub-claim REFUTED on the count: rs-dpp state_transition_types.rs and docs.dash.org protocol-ref/shielded-pool.html both define SIX shielded transition types, not five: Shield=15, ShieldedTransfer=16, Unshield=17, ShieldFromAssetLock=18, ShieldedWithdrawal=19, IdentityCreateFromShieldedPool=20. All five named types exist with those names; the claim omits the sixth. The docs state the pool uses Zcash's Orchard shielded protocol with Halo 2 zero-knowledge proofs.

**Corrected.** Dash Platform DAPI (per the "latest" docs.dash.org reference and the gRPC platform.proto on dashpay/platform's default branch v4.1-dev; not yet in the "stable" docs) exposes getTotalCreditsInPlatform plus SIX shielded endpoints: getShieldedPoolState, getShieldedNotesCount, getMostRecentShieldedAnchor, getShieldedAnchors, getShieldedEncryptedNotes, and getShieldedNullifiers. The protocol defines SIX (not five) shielded state transition types: Shield (15), Shielded Transfer (16), Unshield (17), Shield from Asset Lock (18), Shielded Withdrawal (19), and Identity Create From Shielded Pool (20). The shielded pool uses Zcash's Orchard protocol with Halo 2 zk proofs.

**Sources:**
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/latest/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints.html (primary, official docs; raw HTML grepped)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/latest/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints-platform-endpoints.html (primary; anchors confirmed via raw HTML of overview page links)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/latest/docs/protocol-ref/shielded-pool.html (primary; lists six transition types 15-20, Orchard/Halo 2)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/stable/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints-platform-endpoints.html (primary; stable version lacks shielded endpoints — versioning caveat)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/v4.1-dev/packages/dapi-grpc/protos/platform/v0/platform.proto (primary; gRPC service definition, default branch)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/v4.1-dev/packages/rs-dpp/src/state_transition/state_transition_types.rs (primary; enum with six Shield* variants = 15..20)
- GitHub code search API: search/code q=getShieldedPoolState repo:dashpay/platform → 22 files (primary)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/master/packages/dapi-grpc/protos/platform/v0/platform.proto (primary; master branch has getTotalCreditsInPlatform but 0 shielded mentions — feature lives on v4.1-dev/latest)

## 3. PARTIALLY-CONFIRMED (high)

**Утверждение.** Around 4.4-5 million ZEC (~30% of circulating supply) currently sits in the Zcash shielded pool, up from ~8% in early 2024.

**Evidence.** Live on-chain data as of 2026-07-19 (block 3,418,066): total shielded = 4,385,630.68 ZEC (Sprout 25,409.42 + Sapling 590,550.66 + Orchard 3,769,670.60) against total supply 16,830,361.10 ZEC = 26.06% — NOT ~30%. The Orchard figure (3,769,670.59687407 ZEC) is byte-identical across two independent explorers (zkp.baby and mainnet.zcashexplorer.app), and ZecHub's on-chain dataset shows 4,384,396 ZEC / 16,830,102 = 26.05% the same day. The ~30% figure is ~6 weeks stale: ZecHub weekly series shows shielded supply crossed 5.0M/30% in late Jan 2026, peaked at ~5.17-5.21M (~31.0%) on 2026-04-25, held ~5.1M (30.6%) through May 30, 2026 (matching the crypto.news article of May 29, 2026 that the dialog cites), then dropped ~750K ZEC in early June 2026 (4.37M by June 13) to ~26% where it remains. The tonnage part of the claim is marginally right: 4.386M rounds to 4.4M, the bottom edge of the stated 4.4-5M range. The historical baseline is CONFIRMED: ZecHub series shows 1,280,000 ZEC = 8.78% (2024-01-01), 8.58% (Feb 2024), 8.04% (Mar 2024); trajectory corroborated at 10.8% (Nov 17, 2024), 23% (Nov 2025, The Block), plus a Wayback-archived explorer snapshot (2024-06-24: Orchard pool 378,878.87 ZEC). Supply figure independently validated by emission math (15.75M at second halving + 691,673 blocks x 1.5625 ZEC = 16.83M, exact match). Note: Blockchair's ZEC circulation field returns a negative/broken value and was used only for block-height/date corroboration.

**Corrected.** As of 2026-07-19, about 4.39 million ZEC (~26% of the ~16.83M total supply) sits in Zcash shielded pools. The pool did reach ~5.0-5.2M ZEC (~30-31% of supply) between late January and early June 2026, peaking in late April 2026, but a ~750K ZEC outflow in early June 2026 brought it back to ~4.4M (~26%). The historical baseline is accurate: shielded supply was ~1.19-1.28M ZEC, i.e. ~8-8.8% of supply, in early 2024.

**Sources:**
- https://zkp.baby/ (primary - live chain state, all pools, block 3418066)
- https://mainnet.zcashexplorer.app/ (primary - live explorer, Orchard pool, block 3418068)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZecHub/zechub-wiki/main/public/data/zcash/shielded_supply.json (primary - ZecHub on-chain historical series 2017-2026, updated 2026-07-19)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZecHub/zechub-wiki/main/public/data/zcash/total_supply.json (primary - ZecHub total supply series)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZecHub/zechub-wiki/main/public/data/zcash/shieldedStatsJSON.json (primary - ZecHub weekly metrics Nov 2024-Jul 2026)
- https://api.blockchair.com/zcash/stats (primary API - block height/date corroboration only; its ZEC circulation field is broken/negative)
- http://web.archive.org/web/20240624101157/https://mainnet.zcashexplorer.app/ (primary, archived - Orchard 378,878.87 ZEC on 2024-06-24)
- https://crypto.news/why-30-of-zcash-supply-is-now-in-the-shielded-pool/ (secondary - May 29, 2026; origin of the claim's 30%/8% figures)
- https://www.theblock.co/post/378232/zcash-shielded-pool-climbs-23-supply-network-usage-surges (secondary - Nov 11, 2025, 23% datapoint)
- https://zcashtracker.com/charts/shielded-zcash (secondary - undated 'roughly 30%' text, no numbers)

## 4. PARTIALLY-CONFIRMED (high)

**Утверждение.** There is currently (as of ~2026-07-19) no full-featured public on-chain analytics service ("Glassnode for Dash Platform") tracking Dash Platform metrics such as shielded pool balance, Identities, Data Contracts, Documents, and Credits.

**Evidence.** The narrow reading ("no Glassnode-class analytics suite for Dash Platform") holds: Glassnode lists Dash only under market-metrics coverage (no Platform L2 on-chain metrics); Blockchair covers Dash L1 only (blocks/transactions/addresses, one of 16 supported mainnets); Coin Metrics covers Dash L1; Dune has no Dash Platform coverage; dashradar.com is defunct (last activity ~2018). BUT the claim's enumeration is refuted: the public, open-source Dash Platform Explorer (platform-explorer.com, backend API at platform-explorer.pshenmic.dev, repo pshenmic/platform-explorer, v2.4.0 released 2026-07-13) tracks ALL five named metrics on mainnet, verified via live API calls on 2026-07-19: /status returned 3,038 identities, 59 data contracts, 17,537 documents, total credits in circulation 2,674,661,404,651,987, epoch 75, block height 401,743, indexer 100% synced; /transactions/shielded/statistic returned shielded poolBalance = 5,010,482,208,400 credits (totalShieldedIn 5,136,307,782,000; totalShieldedOut 125,825,573,600; 133 shielded transitions) — i.e., it already tracks the shielded pool balance only 2 days after Dash activated Orchard-based shielded pools on the Evolution chain (July 17, 2026, per multiple concordant news reports of the official Dash X announcement). It also exposes historical time-series endpoints (identities history, transactions history, gas history, shield/unshield history, validator stats, epochs, richlist, data-contract ratings). Additionally, mnowatch.org/evonodes tracks the L1 Credit Pool (asset-lock) balance and evonode economics. What Platform Explorer is NOT: a Glassnode-style analytics platform with a large curated metric catalog, entity clustering, dashboards, and market intelligence — it is an explorer with a growing stats/time-series API and basic charts.

**Corrected.** Accurate version: There is no Glassnode-class, full-featured analytics platform dedicated to Dash Platform — major providers (Glassnode, Coin Metrics, Blockchair, Dune) cover only Dash L1 or market data. However, it is wrong that the named metrics are untracked: the free, open-source Dash Platform Explorer (platform-explorer.com, actively maintained, v2.4.0 of 2026-07-13) publicly tracks all of them on mainnet — Identities (3,038), Data Contracts (59), Documents (17,537), Credits in circulation (~2.67 quadrillion), epochs, validators, richlist — and, as of the Orchard shielded-pool activation on Dash Evolution (July 17, 2026), already exposes the shielded pool balance (~5.01 trillion credits, 133 transitions) plus shield/unshield history via its API. mnowatch.org additionally tracks the L1 Credit Pool (asset-lock) balance. The genuine gap is Glassnode-style depth (curated metric catalog, dashboards, entity/cohort analytics), not basic tracking of these metrics.

**Sources:**
- https://platform-explorer.pshenmic.dev/status — primary (live mainnet API, identity/contract/document/credits counts)
- https://platform-explorer.pshenmic.dev/transactions/shielded/statistic — primary (live shielded pool balance in credits)
- https://platform-explorer.com — primary (mainnet explorer UI; JS-rendered, charts for Jun-Jul 2026, richlist)
- https://platform-explorer.com/api — primary (API docs: shield/unshield history, shielded statistic, identities history endpoints)
- https://github.com/pshenmic/platform-explorer — primary (open source, 38 releases, v2.4.0 dated 2026-07-13)
- https://mnowatch.org/evonodes/ — primary (evonode registry, L1 Credit Pool / asset-lock balance tracking)
- https://blockchair.com/dash — primary (Dash L1 explorer/analytics only; no Platform L2 data)
- https://docs.glassnode.com/data/supported-assets/market-metrics-coverage — primary (Dash in market-metrics coverage; on-chain coverage page returned 404 on fetch)
- https://coverage.coinmetrics.io/ — primary, seen via search only (Dash L1 coverage portal)
- https://www.cryptopolitan.com/dash-launch-zcash-orchard-technology/ — secondary (Orchard shielded pools activated on Dash Evolution July 17, 2026)
- https://www.tradingview.com/news/cointelegraph:e0f932e12094b:0-dash-evolution-chain-integrates-zcash-orchard-privacy-pool/ — secondary (Cointelegraph syndication, corroborates activation)
- https://crypto-economy.com/dash-activates-orchard-privacy-system-using-zcash-tech-to-hide-transactions/ — secondary (corroborates official X announcement, 1-second confirmations)
- https://www.dash.org/forum/threads/interactive-blockchain-visualization-tool-development-thread.15425/ — secondary (dashradar history, ~2018, now defunct)

## 5. CONFIRMED (high)

**Утверждение.** DAPI/Dash Platform only exposes CURRENT state for shielded pool metrics — no historical/height-parameterized query and no history endpoint — so building a time series requires taking your own periodic snapshots.

**Evidence.** 

**Corrected.** Essentially as stated, with two refinements: (1) a getShieldedPoolState endpoint DOES exist (added in Dash Platform v4.0.0, one of six shielded RPCs) — but it accepts no height/timestamp parameter (request carries only a `prove` flag) and returns only the current total shielded balance, so the substantive point stands: no historical/height-parameterized query and no pool-metrics history endpoint exist, and DAPI consumers wanting a time series must snapshot the current value themselves (the response's metadata.height stamps when each snapshot was taken). (2) Per official docs (~2026-07), the shielded endpoints are defined in the protocol but "not yet available on public nodes," so even current-state queries may not be live on public masternodes yet.

**Sources:**
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/v4.1-dev/packages/dapi-grpc/protos/platform/v0/platform.proto (primary — gRPC service + message definitions, shielded RPCs lines 123–134, GetShieldedPoolStateRequest lines 2952–2957)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/stable/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints.html (primary — official endpoint list incl. Shielded Transactions section)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/stable/_sources/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints.md.txt (primary — docs source; 'Added in Dash Platform v4.0.0'; 'not yet available on public nodes' notice)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/v4.1-dev/packages/rs-sdk/src/platform/types/shielded.rs (primary — FetchCurrent/fetch_current_no_parameters implementation)
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dashpay/platform/v4.1-dev/packages/js-evo-sdk/src/shielded/facade.ts (primary — poolState() takes no args)
- GitHub code search API repo:dashpay/platform (primary — 'shielded' 548 hits; 'GetShieldedPoolStateAtHeight' 0 hits; 'shielded history' hits are wallet-local only)
- https://docs.dash.org/projects/platform/en/latest/docs/reference/dapi-endpoints-platform-endpoints.html (primary — detailed Platform endpoints page; shielded detail sections not yet published there)
